titleStmt.title: ኢሮብ፡, Gunda Gunde, MS GG no. 00044
Taʾammǝra Māryām

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This XML file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

supportDesc.foliation: There is foliation provided in the bottom part of each folio.

binding.decoNote: The manuscript seems to have been rebound by the library.

history.provenance: The manuscript belonged to the monastery of Gunda Gunde, east Tigray, Ethiopia.

msItem.title: Taʾammǝra Māryām
Introductory text and hymn to the Miracles of Mary
Introductive exhortation to the Taʾammǝra Māryām
ʾƎsaggǝd laki ʾǝsaggǝd laki ʾǝsaggǝd laki wa-ʾǝweddǝsaki
Hymn to Our Lady Mary

Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the preservation of the Church of the Virgin in ʾAṯrīb
Taʾammǝra fǝqrǝki
Miracle of Mary: Homily on a potter from Syria
Hymn to Mary
Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary made a stream to reverse its course
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the drunk monk Timotewos (?), saved by the Virgin
Miracle of Mary: How Nicodemus the Persian knight became a Christian
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Roman prefect hindered of entering the Holy Sepulchre
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Bishop Philoteus
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a fountain of water caused by Jesus in Māḫḍāb while returning to Palestine
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Yoḥannǝs Kǝmā who founded a Convent
Prayer of Our Lady Mary for the 16th Yakkātit
Sǝnkǝssār Commemoration of Mary for the 16th Yakkātit

layout.ab: Primary pricks and horizontal pricks are visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.

decoDesc.summary: The manuscript is richly decorated with multiple miniatures mainly belong to the so called Second Gondarine style of decoration. Paintings in the manuscript appear after their corresponding narrations as means of illustration for the texts.

desc.ref: Miracle of Mary: Daqsǝyos
Miracle of Mary: Mary speaks to a worker
Miracle of Mary: The Jew of ʾAkmim
Miracle of Mary: Dǝmyanos who wrote Mary's name in golden ink
Miracle of Mary: ʾAbbās, bishop of Rome
Miracle of Mary: The monk, Yǝsḥaq, who prayed for seven years that St Mary would appear to him
Miracle of Mary: Māryām of Dafrā
Miracle of Mary: The French artist who fell from the scaffolding while painting frescoes
Miracle of Mary: Mary carries a sick man to Jerusalem
Miracle of Mary: Zakāryās, who used to bring roses to the icon of St Mary
Miracle of Mary: Yolyānā and Barbārā from Bethlehem
Miracle of Mary: The three Arabs journeying to Rif and Salomon, the abbot of Qalamon
Miracle of Mary: The church of ʿƎqonā, which was removed to the edge of the sea
Miracle of Mary: The blind priest, Yoḥannǝs Bakansi
Miracle of Mary: The rich man from Qʷalāsis who fought the Qʷǝz and whose eye was pierced by an arrow
Miracle of Mary: Elizabeth, the blind daughter of Abraham and Gerā ʾAnǝśt of Badǝrmān
Miracle of Mary: The widow who could not find husbands for her three daughters
Miracle of Mary: The two brothers who copied the Miracles of Mary
Miracle of Mary: Giyorgis Ḥaddis, who was delivered from prison
Miracle of Mary: Kaṭir, the old priest at ʾƎlkǝsus
Miracle of Mary: The two brothers Tāg and Nazib from Dalgā who were dyers
Miracle of Mary: The stone-footed man from France
Miracle of Mary: Bishop Mercurius, whom the patriarch wanted to remove because of leprosy
Miracle of Mary: The woman with a broken foot from Hartarom
Miracle of Mary: Sophia, abbess of Mount Carmel, who became pregnant
Miracle of Mary: Bārok of Finǝqi whom his enemies could not kill until he had confessed himself to a priest
Miracle of Mary: The deacon ʾAnǝsṭāsyos who recited the prayer tafaśśǝḥi and was healed by Mary
Miracle of Mary: The monk of Dabra Qalǝmon who did not fast
Miracle of Mary: The cannibal of Qǝmǝr
Miracle of Mary: The son of a widow, who became a thief, is saved by Mary
Miracle of Mary: The pregnant woman who was caught by the tide while going to the church of St Michael
Miracle of Mary: Mary gives a thirsty dog water to drink from her shoe
Covenant of Mercy

item.desc: In each incipits and explicits, the name ʾErāqlis is provided as the copyist's name of the ʾAkkonu Bǝʾǝsi collection of the codicological unit.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: The beginning of the history of the Ark of Zion in the giving of the Ten Commandments

layout.ab: Primary pricks and horizontal pricks are visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.

msItem.title: Mǝnbāb za-fǝlsatā

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)
